title: Lucid Group: From Promise to Disappointment
text: Lucid Group faced a significant setback when its newly launched Gravity SUV experienced a rear-seat defect that required a recall in February, disrupting deliveries at a critical moment. The timing proved particularly unfortunate since Tesla had discontinued competing models. The company's first-quarter results revealed troubling trends. Net losses ballooned to $1 billion compared to $366 million a year prior, while revenue of $282 million fell dramatically short of Wall Street's $440 million estimate. This represented the largest shortfall in more than four years. Despite management's optimism about a 2027 production ramp-up, Lucid faces pressing concerns. The automaker ended Q1 with approximately $3.2 billion in liquidity while burning cash at an unsustainable rate. In response, leadership implemented a 12% workforce reduction expected to generate up to $500 million in savings over three years. The recurring pattern of production disruptions continues undermining investor confidence. What appeared poised for growth has instead morphed into another disappointing chapter for this struggling EV manufacturer.
